brief.headline: Total portfolio value at stake: 23,519,703.73 AED across 294 flagged SKUs — immediate action required
brief.body_markdown:
## Portfolio Value-at-Stake Summary

A diagnostic run across 600 SKUs has identified **23,519,703.73 AED** of total value at stake, decomposed across three risk dimensions. Management attention is required on two executable release actions and one urgent replenishment intervention.

---

### Largest Release Opportunity — Slow & Excess Inventory

The single largest lever is the slow-excess cluster, comprising 191 SKUs confirmed safe-to-release, with excess units sitting above order-up-to levels. Executing supplier returns, inter-company transfers, or secondary-channel markdowns against this cluster would unlock **18,819,464.91 AED** in trapped working capital. All SKUs in this cluster have been validated against service-level guardrails and carry no stockout risk; ERP parameter updates and supplier sign-off represent the only execution dependencies. The CFO should prioritise this action first given its scale and immediate feasibility.

---

### Expiry Write-Off Exposure

The expiry cluster covers 30 near-expiry Pharma SKUs carrying a write-off exposure of **280,657.13 AED**. Emergency sell-through and supplier credit claims can be initiated within days, subject to regulatory dispensing approvals and shelf-life return clauses. Inaction will convert this exposure directly into P&L write-offs; this is therefore the second priority action for the commercial and supply-chain teams.

---

### Stockout Margin-Loss Risk

The stockout cluster — 84 SKUs — represents **4,419,581.69 AED** of margin at risk from lost sales. Every SKU in this cluster is at or below its order-up-to level; releasing any stock would breach service-level targets. This cluster is excluded from the release plan. The COO must instead direct emergency replenishment and dual-sourcing actions to arrest ongoing margin erosion.

---

### Summary Table

| Risk Dimension | Value at Stake (AED) | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Slow & excess — releasable cash | 18,819,464.91 | Release (first priority) |
| Expiry — write-off exposure | 280,657.13 | Emergency sell-through (second priority) |
| Stockout — margin loss at risk | 4,419,581.69 | Emergency replenishment (excluded from release) |
| **Total** | **23,519,703.73** | |

Across the full portfolio of 600 SKUs, 294 have been flagged for intervention.

prioritise: release item for cluster 'expiry' breaches the guardrail: 20 member(s) not safe to release (e.g. ['PE-0497', 'PE-0502', 'PE-0506'])
